title: Anthocephalus cadamba
taxonomy: Anthocephalus cadamba is classified under the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, it falls under the clade Angiosperms and the class Magnoliopsida. It belongs to the family Rubiaceae and the order Gentianales. Within the family Rubiaceae, Anthocephalus cadamba is part of the tribe Naucleeae. The genus Anthocephalus is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species, which is A. cadamba. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics.
uses: Anthocephalus cadamba is known for its various medicinal properties and uses. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. Its bark and leaves are also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extracts have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation.
